findings.title: statusline lacks the path-traversal guard its sibling hooks have; AGENT with '/' or '..' writes .ctx to an arbitrary path
findings.detail: keeper-precompact-hook.sh (L85-87) and keeper-sessionstart-hook.sh (L65-67) both reject agent names matching '*/*|*..*' before using them in a file path. keeper-statusline.sh derives AGENT the same way (env HARMONIK_AGENT, or the tmux session name via `tmux display-message -p '#S'`, or 'default') but performs NO such guard before building CTX_FILE="${CTX_DIR}/${AGENT}.ctx" (L65) and writing/renaming to it (L142-143). A tmux session name or env value containing '/' or '..' (e.g. AGENT='../../../tmp/x') causes the atomic write+mv to land outside .harmonik/keeper. Because the statusLine command runs on every UI repaint, this is the most-frequently-executed of the four hooks. Inconsistent hardening within the same skill family is itself a maintainability hazard: a reader reasonably assumes all four scripts share the guard.
findings.suggested_fix: Add the same `case "$AGENT" in */*|*..*) ... ;; esac` guard (skip write / exit 0) immediately after AGENT derivation, mirroring precompact/sessionstart.

findings.title: stop-hook lacks the path-traversal guard its sibling hooks have; AGENT with '/' or '..' touches an arbitrary path
findings.detail: keeper-stop-hook.sh derives AGENT from HARMONIK_AGENT / HARMONIK_KEEPER_AGENT / $1 / tmux session name / 'default' (L43-53) then builds IDLE_FILE="${KEEPER_DIR}/${AGENT}.idle" (L56) and `touch`es it (L59) with no validation. Its siblings keeper-precompact-hook.sh and keeper-sessionstart-hook.sh both reject '*/*|*..*' agent names for exactly this file path. A crafted agent name (via $1 positional, env, or a tmux session name) makes the hook create/mtime-bump an arbitrary file outside .harmonik/keeper. Same guard-inconsistency class as the statusline finding.
findings.suggested_fix: Add the `case "$AGENT" in */*|*..*) exit 0 ;; esac` guard after AGENT derivation, matching precompact/sessionstart.

findings.title: HARMONIK_KEEPER_1M_EFFECTIVE_FRACTION is interpolated unescaped into an awk program (code-injection / crash surface)
findings.detail: L120-121: `_1M_FRACTION="${HARMONIK_KEEPER_1M_EFFECTIVE_FRACTION:-0.5}"` then `WINDOW_SIZE="$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%d\n\", 1000000 * ${_1M_FRACTION}}")"`. The value is spliced directly into the awk source with no numeric validation. A non-numeric value injects awk syntax (e.g. `0.5} END {system("...")` style) or simply produces a syntax error / 0, silently corrupting WINDOW_SIZE and thus the keeper's fill gauge. Every other numeric input in this script (TOKENS L95, WINDOW_SIZE L96, the override L101) is grep-validated as an integer first; this one is not. It is operator-controlled env, hence low, but it is an avoidable unvalidated-into-interpreter path.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate _1M_FRACTION against `^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$` before use (fall back to 0.5 on mismatch), or pass it via `awk -v f="$_1M_FRACTION"` instead of string interpolation.

findings.title: statusline omits the HARMONIK_KEEPER_AGENT backward-compat alias that all three other hooks honor
findings.detail: precompact (L73-76), sessionstart (L55-58), and stop (L43-46) all fall back HARMONIK_AGENT -> HARMONIK_KEEPER_AGENT before tmux. keeper-statusline.sh (L53-59) checks only HARMONIK_AGENT then tmux, skipping HARMONIK_KEEPER_AGENT. An operator relying on the documented backward-compat alias gets a consistent agent name for the .idle/.sid/.precompact channels but a divergent one for .ctx, so the keeper's identity-comparison (.sid PRIMARY vs .ctx gauge) can key off two different agent names in the same session.
findings.suggested_fix: Add the HARMONIK_KEEPER_AGENT elif branch to statusline's AGENT derivation so all four scripts resolve identically.

findings.title: TMP_FILE (.ctx.tmp.$$) is orphaned if the script dies between write and rename
findings.detail: L66 TMP_FILE="${CTX_FILE}.tmp.$$"; L142 writes it; L143 `mv` renames. With `set -euo pipefail` and no trap, any failure of the printf write (disk full, EIO) after the file is partially created — or a signal between L142 and L143 — leaves a stale `.ctx.tmp.<pid>` in .harmonik/keeper. Because the statusline runs on every repaint with a fresh PID each time, these can accumulate over a long session. Minor, but a `trap 'rm -f "$TMP_FILE"' EXIT` would make the atomic-write pattern actually clean up after itself.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an EXIT trap removing TMP_FILE, or `rm -f` stale `${CTX_FILE}.tmp.*` at start.
